dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a reported unknown problem/person down at --Address Redacted--.
Hi, I'm EMT Alex. What made you call 911 today?
She has diabetes and was acting weak, with signs of hyperglycemia noted.
She's alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation; GCS of 15.
Airway is patent and easily maintained. No signs of obstruction observed.
She is breathing at 18 per minute and shows no signs of respiratory distress.
Peripheral pulses are present, and her skin is warm with normal capillary refill.
Blood glucose is 533. Has there been similar readings recently?
Skin appears normal with no rashes or lesions noted during the exam.
Head and neck assessment shows no trauma or swelling.
No drainage, redness, or swelling seen in the HEENT structures.
Clear bilateral breath sounds with no wheezes or crackles detected.
ECG monitoring is in place and shows sinus tachycardia.
Abdomen is flat with no rigidity, guarding, or deformities.
Pelvis is intact and stable with no signs of trauma observed.
Blood pressure is 146 over 84, pulse is 154, and SpO2 is at 99%.
Have you noticed any new or changing symptoms in the last 24 hours?
She had similar symptoms last week at [COMPANY_NAME]-weakness, but no pain or vomiting.
All extremities show full movement and pulses remain palpable.
Are you experiencing any chest discomfort or difficulty breathing?
She hasn't had chest pain or shortness of breath, but has had some dizziness.
Her medical history includes diabetes, bipolar disorder, depression, and anxiety.
She has a history of anogenital herpes. Has she had any recent outbreaks?
She is currently taking Lantus, NovoLog, and Seroquel.
Yes, she has taken all her medications as prescribed.
She has a known allergy to sulfonamides. Has there been any recent exposure?
To the best of our knowledge, she has had no contact with sulfonamides recently.
Pulse decreased to 135, respiratory rate is 16, and blood pressure is 136/93.
She is breathing at 16 with no signs of distress observed.
4-lead ECG placed. Current rhythm is sinus tachycardia.
Blood glucose remains unchanged at 533.
Transporting her in a 5-point harness with semi-Fowler's positioning for comfort.
This position should optimize her breathing and maintain comfort during transport.
Respiratory rate now recorded at 16, and responsive with no distress.
An IV has been placed in her extremity with gauge selected per procedure.
ECG will continue to monitor for any changes in sinus tachycardia during transport.
Following Protocol 3-8 for diabetic hyperglycemia as appropriate per ePCR.
Blood pressure is now at 136/93, pulse is 135 and SpO2 remains at 100%.
Transport destination is CMC-ED Trauma Bed 2. ED has been advised.
IV line is secure and continues to function as intended.
Glucose remains elevated at 533, and the patient is stable.
She is now secured and ready for transport with all conditions stable.
Exploring arrival in five minutes to CMC-ED. Monitoring will continue en route.
She is still alert with no signs of distress and vitals show no changes.
Transport initiated according to protocol. Patient remains stable.
Anticipated arrival at CMC-ED in three minutes. ED has received call.
Monitoring is ongoing. All transport conditions are stable.
Respiration is steady at 16 with no signs of bronchoconstriction or altered mental status.
Five-point harness is secure and ECG is still active.
Pre-handoff preparations are complete; monitor data has been shared with ED.
All systems and monitoring devices are active and functioning properly for travel.
Blood pressure is still 136/93, no acute changes observed.
Arrival at CMC-ED is less than two minutes away, all vitals are stable.
Documentation completed. Handover will happen at CMC-ED.
Patient remains stable and all protocol procedures have been maintained.
No complications or ongoing changes have been reported as of now.
She remains alert with no change in her current physiological status.
ECG shows continued sinus tachycardia and no signs of arrhythmia.
Harness removed. Patient is now on the trauma stretcher inside the hospital.
Arrival and handoff are about to begin.
Final check shows no changes over the past 5 minutes. Pt in same condition.
Patient is ready to be transferred at this point. No additional hands-on required.
ED has been advised and is expecting the patient with all vital status matters updated.
Vital signs remain per original documentation with no acute variations reported.
Patient is now being transferred and conditions remain unchanged.
ED confirmed intake and standing by for the patient.
Patient is now safely placed on the assigned trauma bed at CMC.
All transport protocols followed correctly as per facility procedures.
ED is now organizing for patient intake per protocol without incident.
Receiving team is humbled and all relevant history is relayed.
Final handover timestamp noted.
All interventions and monitoring were passed on as part of documentation.
No emergent alterations during deployment observed. No event requiring stop.
No problem has occurred during or upon transport and arrival phases.
Medications and protocols were audited for travel requirements.
Patient handed over properly as per hospital protocol and confirmed safe checklist.
Patient is now in the trauma bed as assigned by ED. No further action required.
No serious request or further action needed from ED at this time.
Transport process followed field document and ED noted arrival without issue.
Transport bench was weighed and logged for departure as per field protocol.
Patient location confirmed as per ED hospital log.
Equipment was assessed correctly per standard procedure before team departure.
Oxygenation remains stable throughout entire hospital arrival.
IV line is intact and shows no concerning fluctuations.
Monitoring was consistent during EDC without issue noted.
Continuous EMS supervision during transfer as per patient need and protocol.
ED EMS takeover handed off as updated.
No dissatisfaction observed during ED transfer for either end.
All logistical operations were carried out as expected.
Communication was disseminated between EMS team and ED as intended.
Transfer to trauma center completed. No issues logged at time of transfer.
Transport guidelines followed allowing crew to return as per unit policy.
No re-transport is necessary with ED intake completed and active.
All exchange communication was noted between EMS field and ED on arrival.
No delays recorded from initial dispatch to hospital delivery.
No significant fluctuation in vitals recorded during transport.
No further instructions requested on arrival from ED.
All key deliverables met and EMS process transitioning to dispatch.
